source_title: Thermal-Mechanical Delamination for Recovery of Tempered Glass from Photovoltaic Panels; Solvent versus thermal treatment for glass recovery from end of life photovoltaic panels: Environmental and economic assessment
page_or_table: Studies report contamination and low-value glass fractions from conventional treatment, while improved delamination can recover high-grade glass; neither source specifies an exact 30 percent discount
notes: The 30 percent central discount is retained as an allowance for contamination, breakage, encapsulant residues, sorting requirements and possible downcycling. The actual value depends on the recovery process and output purity. This is a price-quality adjustment, not a material recovery loss.

source_title: The Design Value for Recycling End-of-Life Photovoltaic Panels; Recovery and analysis of polymeric layers from waste solar modules by chemical route

The actual discount depends strongly on recovery technology and polymer purity. This is a price-quality adjustment and not a material recovery loss.

source_title: The Green Book 2026; Green Book supplementary guidance on discounting
page_or_table: Official guidance requires consistency between real monetary values and real discount rates. It applies 3.5 percent for years 1-30 and 3.0 percent for years 31-75; the fixed 3 percent rate used here is a study sensitivity case
notes: Low discount-rate case for testing the sensitivity of long-term material values. It is not presented as an India-specific official discount rate.

source_title: The Green Book 2026; Green Book supplementary guidance on discounting
page_or_table: Official guidance demonstrates real discounting and declining long-term rates but does not prescribe a fixed 5 percent rate for this application. The 5 percent value is the study-defined central sensitivity case
notes: Central discount-rate case used to report the main present-value results. It is not an official social discount rate or a measured recycling-industry cost of capital.

source_title: The Green Book 2026; Green Book supplementary guidance on discounting
page_or_table: Official guidance illustrates that the discount-rate choice strongly affects long-term values but does not prescribe a fixed 7 percent rate for this application. The 7 percent value is a conservative high-rate sensitivity case
notes: High discount-rate case used to test how strongly delayed material values are reduced. It is not presented as an India-specific official rate.
